title: String literal passed as non-const pointer may cause undefined behavior
problem:
When ctx->pass == NULL, the code passes (unsigned char *)DEFAULT_PASS to SDF_DL_GetPrivateKeyAccessRight. DEFAULT_PASS is a string literal defined in provider.h as "#define DEFAULT_PASS "12345678"", which is stored in read-only memory. The cast to unsigned char * removes the const qualifier. If the SDF SDK modifies the password buffer, it will cause undefined behavior (segmentation fault or memory corruption).

This is inconsistent with provider.c which properly allocates a heap buffer and copies the default password (lines 209-220).
